Narrow L1 cleaned-to-null rate to pipeline-only filtering

Silence-token detections (model choosing not to speak) and dedup drops
(model repeating itself) are not pipeline failures, so exclude both from
the L1 cleaned-to-null rate check. Add pipeline_filter_count as matched
denominator to prevent dilution by benign events.

commit.message:
Implement module-aware LSP (#10)

* Implement module-aware LSP

**Major Changes:**
1. Persisted compiler-owned project-root, navigation, and module analysis state so LSP features can consume module-aware facts instead of reconstructing them ad hoc.
2. Added module-aware typed feature parity for hover, signature help, code actions, semantic-token/editor scenarios, and go-to-definition across direct imports, aliases, namespace members, and import headers.
3. Implemented parser-tolerant module and import completion with module catalog scanning, source overrides, last-good fallback, and namespace-aware dot completion.
4. Updated the CLI entrypoints to call the thunked compiler build/check APIs introduced during the module-aware compiler work.

**Testing:**
✅ make unit
✅ make integration
✅ make ci-lsp-integration
✅ make ci-editor-vscode
✅ make ci-editor-nvim
✅ make ci-editor-jetbrains
✅ make ci-tree-sitter
⚠️ make ci-editor-zed (fails on existing keyword.import capture assertion)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Fix PR follow-ups for module-aware LSP

**Major Changes:**
1. Applied the repository formatter to the module-aware LSP implementation files so the quality gate matches the committed sources again.
2. Moved the completed module-aware LSP plan from todo to done as  and updated the roadmap tooling milestones.
3. Recorded the post-PR follow-up verification in the historical plan progress log.

**Testing:**
✅ make ci-quality-lint-fmt-doc
✅ make unit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix(frontend): guard mock auth behind DEV mode

Summary:
- gate `isMockAuthEnabled` behind `import.meta.env.DEV`
- add a regression test for `DEV=false` with `VITE_ENABLE_MOCKS=true`
- make the existing mock-auth tests explicitly exercise DEV mode

Rationale:
- prevent production builds from enabling mock auth through an env misconfiguration
- lock the security boundary in tests so the production-only failure mode stays covered

Tests:
- npm run test:run
- npm run build
- VITE_ENABLE_MOCKS=true npm run build
- production preview with seeded `shappar:mock-auth-session` stays on /login

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(readme): 重写 README 首页并补充仓库徽章 (#8)

## 为什么
- 当前 README 有项目背景，但首页扫描路径偏弱，第一次打开仓库时不够直观。
- README 顶部也缺少能快速传达仓库状态的公开信息。

## 做了什么

- 新增 `build`、`deps.rs` 和 `license` 三个 badge，并链接到对应来源页。

repository.name: kat
repository.description: A syntax-highlighting cat built around Tree-sitter.

commit.message:
feat(cli): 支持长输出接入外部 pager (#7)

## Why
- `kat` 之前只会一次性把完整 ANSI 输出写到 stdout，长文件场景下缺少可用的翻页体验。
- 这个改动先把外部 pager 路径做扎实，保持现有 renderer 继续只负责产出 ANSI 文本。

## What
- 新增 `--paging=auto|always|never`，默认 `auto`，并且只在普通渲染模式下评估是否进入 pager。
- 重构 CLI 输出链路：先汇总完整输出，再统一决定直写 stdout 还是交给外部 pager。
- 优先读取 `PAGER`，未设置时默认回退到 `less -R -F -X`，并为自动分页路径保留直接输出回退。
- 补充分页参数解析、自动分页判定和 pager stdin 写入相关测试，并同步更新 README 与架构文档。

## Testing
- `just test`

## Notes
- 关联 issue: #3

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(runtime): enforce visible-only replies and serialized guardrails

Remove ingress summary_answer fallback so only explicit user-visible reply fields can emit outbound text. Serialize reply-policy decision and state updates under per-group lock, with policy recheck before committing agent-dispatch replies, preventing stale-state bypass under concurrent turns. Add mention-safe direct fallback when agent dispatch is unavailable for @bot analyze-style prompts, and extend integration/router tests for summary-only dispatch, concurrency guardrails, and default mention runtime behavior.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(runtime): wire stateful reply path and agent dispatch

Separate agent summary output from user-visible reply, integrate stateful reply policy into orchestrator reply path, emit real direct/agent replies for ingress, and extend trace records with latency/degradation fields. Add focused integration coverage for dispatcher behavior, orchestrator degradation/reply state, and end-to-end runtime reply emission. Update README runtime flow to match current behavior.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(policy): add stateful reply guardrails

Introduce ReplyState and enforce interval/topic/repeated-sender guardrails in ReplyPolicyEngine.decide. Add record_reply helper for orchestrator state updates and extend policy/integration tests for the new behavior.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(validation): repair backend and desktop test suites

Summary:
- restore backend test portability by removing listener dependencies
  from analytics and API router tests
- fix MCP invalid JSON handling and align stale backend test
  expectations with current defaults and worktree behavior
- repair desktop typecheck and Vitest fixtures so the renderer
  validation commands pass again

Rationale:
- full validation was still failing after the agent refactor due to
  brittle tests, stale fixtures, and sandbox-sensitive test setup
- moving tests to in-memory handlers makes the suite reliable in CI
  and local sandboxed environments without changing product behavior

Tests:
- cd apps/backend && env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache go test ./...
- cd apps/desktop && npm run typecheck
- cd apps/desktop && npm run test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(frontend): preserve option validation errors on edit

Summary:
- keep untouched option errors when editing a different option on CreatePollPage
- add a regression test covering the three-option failed-submit flow

Rationale:
- the previous handler cleared every option error after any option edit, breaking the
  failed-submit UX
- limiting the reset to the edited index preserves error visibility until the user
  fixes each invalid field

Tests:
- cd frontend && npm run test:run -- src/pages/__tests__/create-poll-page.test.tsx -t
  "keeps validation errors on untouched empty options when another option changes"
- cd frontend && npm run test:run
- cd frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(backend): add claude integration e2e script

Summary:
- add a backend script that drives Orchestra's Claude endpoints end to
  end against a live Claude CLI installation
- cover rule, instruction, settings, skill, sub-agent, MCP, and hook
  flows by writing through the API and verifying the on-disk effect
- include cleanup and restore checks so the script can be used as a
  practical integration probe during local debugging

Rationale:
- the provider-native Claude configuration surface benefits from an
  end-to-end check that proves API writes are actually consumed by the
  real CLI, not just stored on disk
- keeping this as a standalone script makes it easier to run manually
  during backend and desktop integration debugging

Tests:
- not run (script added for manual end-to-end validation)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(api): add contract ownership and domain notes

Summary:
- add repository guidelines in AGENTS.md for local contributors and
  coding agents
- add API contract standards for ownership, versioning, rollout,
  deprecation, query parameters, type generation, and validation
- add per-domain API notes covering core runtime, issues, projects,
  git/github, MCP, sessions, analytics, and workspace/unsandbox
- add schema refactor draft and plan documents for staged contract
  cleanup work

Rationale:
- the new provider-native agent work exposed a broader need for clear
  API contract ownership and migration standards across the repo
- domain notes and standards make later schema and route cleanup
  easier to stage without losing context or duplicating decisions
- keeping this as a docs-only commit avoids mixing it with runtime code
  or test tooling changes

Tests:
- not run (documentation only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(shadow_mode): use decision.action instead of decision.allowed (#109)

* fix(shadow_mode): use decision.action instead of decision.allowed

decision.allowed is never set by _build_decision(); it always sets
decision.action. Reading decision.allowed always yields nil, causing
would_reject=true and original_action='reject' for every request —
including ones that would have been allowed.

Fix: derive was_allowed from decision.action ~= 'reject', which correctly
handles all three states (allow, throttle, reject).

Fixes #108

* fix(shadow_mode): preserve allowed-based compatibility

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(agents): split config UI into provider-native domains

Summary:
- add provider-native backend routes and desktop hooks for Codex,
  Gemini, and OpenCode agent resources
- replace the generic non-Claude agent panels with provider-specific
  Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode panels plus focused frontend tests
- harden backend selection and TUI shutdown so rebuilt backends are
  picked up consistently during local development
- move binary protection into a tracked shared script and fix the
  classifier so valid text files are not blocked as binaries
- document and deprecate the legacy generic config-item routes while
  keeping runtime agent config endpoints intact

Rationale:
- Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode have different config files,
  precedence rules, and resource layouts, so a shared generic editor
  was incorrect and hard to maintain
- provider-native routes and panels align the UI with the real CLI
  config surfaces and remove stale fallback paths that caused fetch
  failures and backend drift issues
- a tracked binary-protection script keeps local hooks and CI aligned,
  and fixes false positives from the old glob-to-regex matcher
- explicit deprecation signals keep the remaining generic compatibility
  routes usable while making the migration path clear

Tests:
- ./scripts/check-binary-protection.sh --repo
- ./scripts/check-binary-protection.sh --staged
- cd apps/backend && env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache go test ./internal/api -run 'Test(GetAgentConfigItems|PostAgentConfigNewRejectsInvalidJSONAndMarksDeprecated|PostAgentConfigUpdateRejectsInvalidJSONAndMarksDeprecated|GetAgentConfig|PatchAgentConfigRejectsInvalidJSON|PostAgentConfigRejectsInvalidJSON)'
- cd apps/backend && env GOCACHE=/tmp/go-build-cache go test ./internal/api -run 'Test(GeminiCommandsCRUD|DeleteGeminiCommandRemovesLegacyMarkdownFile|OpenCodeAgentsCRUD|OpenCodeSkillsCRUD|OpenCodeAgentsProjectWritesScopedDirectory|OpenCodeSkillsProjectWritesScopedDirectory)'
- cd apps/desktop && npx vitest run src/widgets/agents/AgentsDashboard.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/GeminiCommandsPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/GeminiSettingsPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/OpenCodeCommandsPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/OpenCodeConfigPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/OpenCodeInstructionsPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/OpenCodeSkillsPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/CodexApprovalsPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/CodexConfigPanel.test.tsx src/widgets/agents/panels/CodexModelPanel.test.tsx
- cd apps/desktop && npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false | rg 'AgentsDashboard|CodexApprovalsPanel|CodexConfigPanel|CodexModelPanel|GeminiCommandsPanel|GeminiSettingsPanel|OpenCodeCommandsPanel|OpenCodeConfigPanel|OpenCodeInstructionsPanel|OpenCodeSkillsPanel'
- full desktop typecheck still has a pre-existing unrelated failure in src/lib/orchestra-client.test.ts

commit.message:
fix(token_bucket): pass TTL to dict:set to prevent unbounded key accumulation (fixes #106) (#107)

dict:set was called without expiry, so bucket state keys persisted in shared
memory indefinitely. As users come and go, stale entries accumulate until the
dict fills and LRU eviction becomes unpredictable.

TTL = ceil(burst / tokens_per_second) * 2 — the time to fully refill an empty
bucket, doubled. Same pattern as cost_budget.lua line 274.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Clarify missing module member diagnostics

**What:**
- distinguish private module members from missing ones during qualified-name resolution
- report "Module 'x' does not export 'y'" for defined-but-private members
- report "Module 'x' has no member 'y'" when the member is absent
- cover the compiler and LSP module-aware paths with focused regressions

**Why:**
- qualified namespace lookups like sum.reduce were falling through to later phases and surfacing generic unbound-variable-style diagnostics
- the resolver already knows the module surface, so it can produce a precise error earlier

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib --force

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

[raster] Widen edge row interpolation arithmetic

edge_sweep_row() computed the clipped endpoint x coordinates with
int32_t subtractions before casting to int64_t. If an edge started at
INT32_MIN and the scanline clipped endpoint was near zero, ey - yL
overflowed before the slope multiply and UBSan flagged the raster
fuzzer path.

Keep the endpoint interpolation and follow-on dx math in 64-bit until
clamping back to int32_t. This preserves the existing rasterizer logic
while avoiding signed overflow for extreme edge coordinates.

Tested:
- meson test -C build

Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Use compiler diagnostics for module-aware LSP checks

WHY: The LSP was still re-checking open documents as standalone files, so valid module code with imports could show false 'unbound variable' diagnostics even when the compiler accepted it.

WHAT: Detect module-bearing documents in doc_state, resolve their file path from either a URI or a plain path, and delegate diagnostics to the compiler's module-aware entry checker while preserving parsed document state for editor features that do not require inferred types.

TESTING:
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib --force

CHANGES:
- map compiler diagnostics back onto active-file URIs and paths
- route import/export documents through compiler-backed checking
- cover imported-file LSP analysis with a temp-project regression

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Support module checking from unsaved entry buffers

WHY: The compiler could only check on-disk entry files. Editor integrations need module-aware diagnostics against the current buffer contents, especially when imports are involved.

WHAT: Teach discovery to override the entry module source while still reading imported modules from disk, factor graph checking in the frontend compiler, and expose a check_entry_with_source helper for callers with unsaved buffer contents.

TESTING:
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force

CHANGES:
- add entry-source overrides to frontend discovery
- expose module-aware entry checking for unsaved buffers
- cover unsaved imported-entry checking in frontend tests

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

[raster] Move PNG scratch state out of libpng longjmp locals

libpng aborts via longjmp. Our PNG read/write helpers kept scratch
hb_vector_t objects as automatic locals after setjmp and then tried
to free them from the longjmp handler. Once longjmp fires, those
modified locals have indeterminate state, so the cleanup path can
miss live allocations and leak under the raster fuzzer.

Move the PNG scratch buffers and write accumulator into explicit
heap-backed state allocated before setjmp and cleaned up on every
exit path. This keeps both decode and encode cleanup deterministic
across libpng errors.

Tested:
- env LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/home/behdad/harfbuzz/build/src valgrind --leak-check=full --show-leak-kinds=all --error-exitcode=1 ./build/test/fuzzing/hb-raster-fuzzer test/fuzzing/fonts/clusterfuzz-testcase-minimized-hb-subset-fuzzer-5684014636859392
- meson test -C build raster-fuzzer-chunk-0 raster-fuzzer-chunk-1 raster-fuzzer-chunk-2 raster-fuzzer-chunk-3 raster-fuzzer-chunk-4 raster-fuzzer-chunk-5 raster-fuzzer-chunk-6 raster-fuzzer-chunk-7 --wrap='valgrind --leak-check=full --show-leak-kinds=all --error-exitcode=1' --print-errorlogs
- meson test -C build

Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Clear stale Zed grammar cache on pinned installs

WHY: Zed dev installs still failed on main when a previous local grammar setup left tools/zed-marmoset/grammars/marmoset pointing at a file:// clone. Pinned installs expect the GitHub repo and aborted before recompiling the grammar.

WHAT: Teach set-grammar-source.sh to delete an existing grammar cache when pinned mode finds a non-matching origin or a non-git directory, and add a focused CI regression that reproduces the poisoned-cache case.

TESTING:
- ./test/ci/editor-zed.sh

CHANGES:
- clear stale local grammar caches when switching back to pinned mode
- cover the cache-poisoning scenario in editor-zed CI

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

[raster] Fix transformed clip mask intersection bounds

Transformed clip rectangles kept their intersection math in unsigned
loop bounds after clamping only the lower edge to zero. When the
transformed upper edge stayed negative, the signed-to-unsigned cast
wrapped and raster clip reuse read past the previous alpha mask.

The same path also reused emitter-generated clip buffers without
clearing bytes outside the rows it rewrote, which let Valgrind see
uninitialized alpha values on later clip intersections.

Keep the clip intersection in signed ints until after the empty-range
check, and zero newly sized emitter clip buffers before partial writes.

Testing:
- env LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/home/behdad/harfbuzz/build/src valgrind --leak-check=full --error-exitcode=1 ./build/test/fuzzing/hb-raster-fuzzer test/fuzzing/fonts/clusterfuzz-testcase-minimized-hb-raster-fuzzer-4872362293264384
- meson test -C build raster-fuzzer-chunk-0 --wrap='valgrind --leak-check=full --error-exitcode=1' --print-errorlogs
- meson test -C build

Assisted-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: harfbuzz
repository.description: HarfBuzz text shaping engine

commit.message:
Fix Zed dev extension grammar pin

**Major Changes:**
1. Replace the stale unreachable pinned grammar revision in the Zed extension manifest and manifest renderer.
2. Add a Zed CI regression check that fails when the checked-in pinned grammar revision is not reachable from the current repo history.

**Why:**
- Fresh Zed dev extension installs use the checked-in portable manifest, and the previous pinned grammar rev no longer existed in reachable repository history.
- That made Zed fail while installing the dev extension because it could not build the pinned grammar source cleanly from a valid reachable revision.

**Testing:**
- ./test/ci/editor-zed.sh

**Changes:**
- Updated the portable pinned grammar SHA in the checked-in extension manifest and renderer.
- Added a focused editor-zed guard so stale unreachable grammar pins are caught before release.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

[raster] Fail closed on SVG paint stack imbalance

The raster SVG renderer asserted that each element restored the paint
transform, clip, and surface stacks to their entry depths. Fuzzing
found malformed documents that violate that assumption and abort the
process in svg_render_element().

Replace the assertions with explicit stack restoration, discard any
extra group surfaces, and mark the render as failed if the renderer
cannot get back to the saved baseline. The top-level SVG entry point
now checks that failure bit and stops rendering cleanly instead of
crashing.

Testing:
- meson test -C build --suite fuzzing --print-errorlogs
- meson test -C build

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: harfbuzz
repository.description: HarfBuzz text shaping engine

commit.message:
Implement module system (#9)

* Commit plan

* Add module headers and discovery graph groundwork

**Detailed explanation:**
- add `import`, `export`, and `as` to the syntax surface, AST, lowering pipeline, and parser ordering rules so module headers are represented explicitly instead of being hidden in ad hoc text parsing
- add a dedicated `frontend` library with module-graph types plus recursive discovery that resolves module-vs-member imports, detects ambiguity, topologically orders dependencies, and allocates per-file parser ID ranges
- keep the current single-file typecheck helpers building by treating header declarations as metadata-only nodes in existing syntax and typecheck traversals until import resolution/per-module checking lands

**Testing:**
- `dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/syntax --force`
- `dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force`

**Changes:**
- `docs/plans/todo/language/02_module-system.md`: add and update the `PROGRESS` log for the implemented M1/M2 groundwork
- `lib/frontend/syntax/*`: add module-header tokens, AST nodes, parsing, lowering, and parser/lexer tests
- `lib/frontend/module_context.ml` and `lib/frontend/discovery.ml`: add module graph types, cycle detection, ambiguity detection, discovery, and ID-offset tests
- `lib/frontend/typecheck/*`: treat import/export declarations as metadata-only during existing helper traversals

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Implement module compilation pipeline

**Major Changes:**
1. Add a frontend project compiler that discovers reachable modules, resolves semantic imports, rewrites module-visible names to stable internal identifiers, extracts typed module signatures, and compiles modules in dependency order.
2. Wire the CLI build/run/check commands through the entry-file compiler while preserving the legacy single-file fast path for programs without module headers.
3. Expose the new frontend compiler through library boundaries and teach the Go emitter and LSP statement walkers to treat import/export declarations as compile-time-only top-level forms.

**Why:**
- The language plan requires per-module checking, direct-import visibility rules, ambiguity detection, and one flattened Go package across the reachable module graph.
- The previous pipeline only handled single-file programs and could not validate or emit module-based builds.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules modules_edge modules_codegen modules_codegen_edge snapshots

**Changes:**
- Added project compiler/import resolver/signature extraction modules.
- Threaded prepare_state through inference/checker entry points for seeded per-module runs.
- Updated CLI diagnostics lookup for multi-file errors.
- Added import/export exhaustiveness handling in emitter and LSP helpers.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Add module fixtures and snapshot coverage

**Major Changes:**
1. Add a curated multi-module fixture corpus covering namespace imports, direct imports, nested module aliases, imported types/traits/shapes, impl visibility, ambiguity errors, private export rejection, local shadowing, transitive visibility failure, private-name isolation, and Go-keyword collisions.
2. Teach the integration harness to treat  groups as entrypoint suites by running  fixtures only, so support modules are not executed as standalone programs.
3. Add exact emitted-Go snapshots for module import mangling, trait-plus-inherent qualification, and Go-keyword collision cases, and update the module-system plan progress log with the completed implementation and verification notes.

**Why:**
- Module behavior needs regression coverage for both runtime semantics and emitted Go naming/resolution, especially now that imports, visibility, and qualification interact across files.
- Directory-based module fixtures require entrypoint-aware harness behavior to reflect the locked project-root decision.

**Testing:**
- ./test/integration.sh modules modules_edge modules_codegen modules_codegen_edge snapshots
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib --force

**Changes:**
- Added module fixture directories and normalized them to the entry-directory project root rule.
- Added three generated-Go snapshot files plus snapshot-suite coverage.
- Recorded implementation status, findings, and focused verification in the plan doc.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Reject conflicting module import bindings

**Major Changes:**
1. Detect direct-import and namespace-import name collisions instead of silently overwriting earlier bindings during module rewrite.
2. Preserve builtin/direct value precedence when resolving dotted expressions so module namespaces do not steal normal value lookups.
3. Add edge fixtures for conflicting imports and builtin-vs-namespace precedence, plus a resolver unit test for stable qualified-name segment ordering.

**Why:**
- Last-write-wins import binding behavior hid real conflicts and made module resolution order-dependent.
- Qualified namespace lookup must defer to real value bindings at the root segment to preserve normal language semantics.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules_edge

**Changes:**
- Added import collision diagnostics in the module resolver.
- Tightened qualified-name rewriting to respect builtin/direct/current-module values before namespace traversal.
- Added focused modules_edge coverage for these failure modes.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Validate trait impl coherence across module graphs

**Major Changes:**
1. Rewrite reachable modules once up front and reuse those rewrites during project compilation.
2. Pre-register trait impl heads across the whole reachable module graph so duplicate impls are rejected even when they arrive through separate imports.
3. Add focused compiler tests and a multi-file modules_edge fixture for transitive duplicate-impl rejection.

**Why:**
- Per-module checking previously missed conflicting impls that only became visible after flattening the full module graph.
- Module builds need a project-wide coherence pass before typechecking modules independently.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules_edge

**Changes:**
- Added a build-wide rewrite cache and impl registration pass in the frontend compiler.
- Threaded cached module rewrites into per-module compilation.
- Added regression coverage for transitive duplicate impl visibility.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* Stabilize module mangling snapshot coverage

**Major Changes:**
1. Escape module-path components and exported member names when generating internal identifiers so dots, underscores, and sigils stay injective.
2. Add module codegen fixtures for namespace-qualified exported values, nested type qualification, colliding module prefixes, and sigil-name escape collisions.
3. Refresh exact Go snapshots and snapshot-suite coverage to lock the emitted naming and qualification behavior.

**Why:**
- Flattened module builds need internal names that remain distinct across path-shape collisions and escaped user identifiers.
- The new module surface area needs generated-Go regression coverage for both normal namespace qualification and aggressive naming edge cases.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend --force
- ./test/integration.sh modules_codegen modules_codegen_edge snapshots

**Changes:**
- Added escaping for internal module and member identifier components in the import resolver.
- Added three multi-file fixture groups that probe namespace value lowering and mangling collisions.
- Updated exact emitted-Go snapshots and registered them in the snapshot integration suite.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
